task: RESOLUTION TEST A -- make the 2020->2025 vintage leap DECIDABLE, not a bare judgment. Test whether the GCAP holdings-side nationality fraction f (US holdings of CYM/HKG/VGB securities reallocated to CHN nationality), pinned to [0.49,0.71] at 2020 vintage, plausibly TRANSFERS to the 2025-S1 matrix cell -- i.e. whether a plausible 2020->2025 drift could push f down to the break-identification level f*. Land SUPPORTED / UNSUPPORTED / UNDECIDABLE.
compute_artifact: build/audit/vintage_test_compute.json (regenerated by build/audit/vintage_test.py from the inputs above ALONE)

f_path_by_year.basis: Fund Holdings (Methodology 2): ABS-inclusive, MATRIX-COMPARABLE (the CPIS pool carries US-bank ABS via Cayman SPVs); this is the conservative LOW basis. f = sum(Position_Residency * CHN_share) / sum(Position_Residency) over USA x {CYM,HKG,VGB} x asset-class, from the two on-disk GCAP CSVs alone.

years_covered_note: The on-disk denominator CSV covers ALL 14 years 2007-2020 (318 rows: USA x {CYM,HKG,VGB} x methodology x year x asset-class). No missing-year denominators; nothing was fabricated. The prior f_pin_test computed only 2019-2020 for the pin; this test extends to the full path with the same disk inputs.
trend: RISING. Fund Holdings (matrix-comparable) OLS slope = +0.0264 per year over 2007-2020; Enhanced Fund Holdings slope = +0.0337 per year. f climbs from ~0.17-0.19 (2007-08) to 0.53 (2020) [FH] / from 0.28 to 0.71 [EFH]. Monotone-ish upward with small mean-reverting wobbles; never approaches 0.

volatility.basis: Fund Holdings (matrix-comparable, the conservative low basis)
volatility.worst_5yr_note: Over EVERY 5-year window in the 14-year series, f RISES; the worst 5-year outcome is still a +0.068 INCREASE. f has never fallen over any 5-year horizon. The largest single-year move (+0.142, 2016->2017) is an increase; the largest single-year DROP is -0.062 (2015->2016).

break_even_f_star_detail.definition: f* = the f at which the smallest singular value (separation margin) of the column-normalised DP4 15x2 [v_F3|v_F4] operator falls to tau=0.10, at the WORST confound corner s_usd=1.0.

sanity_published_anchors.note: Operator rebuilt from dp4_inputs.json reproduces the published dp4_spectrum anchors EXACTLY (0.078207, 0.28435). f* sits between the f=0.2 anchor (margin 0.078 < tau) and the f=0.49 floor (margin 0.221 > tau), as the task expected.

distance_of_2020_f_from_f_star.reading: The 2020 matrix-comparable f (0.532) sits 0.282 ABOVE the break-identification f* (0.249). Even the pinned FLOOR used by the falsification audit (2019 FH = 0.489) sits 0.240 above f*. To break identification by 2025, f would have to fall by 0.28 (from 2020) or 0.24 (from the pinned floor) in 5 years.

post2020_signal.granularity: COARSER than the f cell. NO publisher has released a post-2020 nationality-restated US-holdings SHARE (the exact f granularity: US holdings of CYM/HKG/VGB securities reallocated to CHN nationality). The GCAP restatement series itself (Restated Bilateral External Portfolios v1.8 / Restatement Matrices v1.5, Coppola-Maggiori-Neiman-Schreger; China in Tax Havens, Clayton-Coppola-Dos Santos-Maggiori-Schreger) all terminate at data year 2020. So at the NEEDED granularity the directional signal is NONE_AT_GRANULARITY.
post2020_signal.direction: UP (at coarser granularity); NONE_AT_GRANULARITY for the exact f share.
what_was_checked: Searched globalcapitalallocation.com / NBER for a GCAP restatement update beyond 2020 (RBEP, Restatement Matrices, China-in-Tax-Havens, GCA Survey w31599). All published restated nationality-basis series end at 2020. NBER w31599 (Florez-Orrego & Maggiori, GCA Survey) and the GCA_Survey.pdf could not be parsed to a post-2020 China-haven SHARE figure; no post-2020 f-granularity data point recovered.

Searched BIS/IMF/NBER for post-2020 China offshore issuance/holdings direction. NBER digest and VoxChina restate the 2020 figures (>60% of haven equity outstanding is Chinese; ~20% of haven corporate bonds; ~$1.24tn US-via-Cayman into China) but add no post-2020 SHARE update.

Cayman Compass (2025-11-03) and IFC Review (2025-11): post-2020 DIRECTIONAL corroboration at coarser granularity -- Chinese FDI stock into Cayman rose to ~$212bn (from $42.3bn in 2013), with $8.7bn added in 2023 (Cayman the largest non-Asian recipient of Chinese FDI in 2023); Chinese companies listed on the three largest US exchanges rose from 250 (2021) to 286 (March 2025, per the US-China Economic and Security Review Commission). Both point to a CONTINUED/DEEPENING China-Cayman channel after 2020 -- consistent with the rising 2007-2020 f path; NOTHING points to a post-2020 REVERSAL downward.

citation.fetch_status: 403 on direct WebFetch; content recovered via search snippet (Cayman Compass; corroborated by IFC Review 2025-11)
citation.signal: Chinese FDI stock into Cayman ~$212bn (up from $42.3bn 2013); +$8.7bn in 2023; Chinese cos on US exchanges 250(2021)->286(Mar 2025). Direction: UP.
Same Nov-2025 reporting, corroborating UP direction.
2020 baseline only (>60% haven equity Chinese; ~20% haven corp bonds; $1.24tn US-via-Cayman). No post-2020 share update -> NONE_AT_GRANULARITY for the exact f cell.

post2020_signal.NONE_AT_GRANULARITY: TRUE for the exact f granularity (post-2020 nationality-restated US-holdings share). The only post-2020 evidence is coarser-granularity directional (FDI stock, US listings), and it points UP, not down.

reason: The f path is STABLE-and-RISING and the 2020 level sits FAR ABOVE the break-identification threshold f*; a plausible 2020->2025 drift, given 14 years of observed volatility, CANNOT reach f*. Evidenced: (1) f* = 0.249 at the worst confound corner (s_usd=1.0); the operator reproduces the published dp4_spectrum anchors exactly. (2) The 2020 matrix-comparable f = 0.532 sits 0.282 ABOVE f*; even the conservative pinned floor (2019 FH = 0.489) sits 0.240 above. (3) To cross f* by 2025, f must fall ~0.28 over 5 years -- but in the ENTIRE 14-year history f never fell over ANY 5-year window (worst 5-year change = +0.068, an INCREASE), the worst single-year drop was only -0.062, and the trend slope is +0.026/yr. A 0.28 fall in 5 years is ~4.5x the worst single-year decline sustained for five straight years with no precedent and against a rising trend. (4) The only grounded post-2020 signal (Cayman Compass / IFC Review, Nov 2025) points UP -- the China-Cayman channel deepened after 2020 (FDI stock to ~$212bn; US-listed Chinese firms 250->286). NOTHING points to a post-2020 reversal. The gap-to-f* (0.24-0.28) lies FAR OUTSIDE the range any plausible 5-year drift has ever covered in this series, so it is NOT UNDECIDABLE. HONEST CAVEAT preserved: this remains a 2020-vintage pin; no publisher has released a 2025 (or any post-2020) nationality-restated US-holdings SHARE at the exact f granularity, so the precise 2025 f value is UNVERIFIED. But the question asked is decidable on the evidence -- the f=0 / f<f* break-identification region is NOT reachable from the 2020 level by any drift this 14-year series has ever exhibited, and the coarse post-2020 signal points the wrong way for a break. Verdict therefore SUPPORTED, not manufactured: stability was tested (full path + volatility + worst-5yr-window), not assumed; the falsifier (a plausible downward drift crossing f*) was explicitly checked against history and against the post-2020 direction, and it does not occur.

decidability_logic.SUPPORTED_if: f path STABLE and FAR ABOVE f* (plausible 2020->2025 drift cannot reach f*) -- THIS CASE.
decidability_logic.UNSUPPORTED_if: f volatile/trending-down/NEAR f* (a plausible drift could cross) -- NOT this case (trend up, gap 0.28 >> any 5yr move).
decidability_logic.UNDECIDABLE_if: no post-2020 signal AND gap-to-f* within plausible-drift range -- NOT this case: gap 0.28 exceeds the worst 5-year move ever observed (+0.068; never negative), and the coarse post-2020 signal points up.

scope_limit: READ-ONLY on prior artifacts. The ONLY files written are build/audit/vintage_test.json (this verdict) and build/audit/vintage_test_compute.json + build/audit/vintage_test.py (the recompute script/raw under build/audit/). dp4_inputs.json, dp4_spectrum.json, f_pin_test.json, china_fraction_bound.json, dp5_* are NOT modified. DP6 NOT started. This test ONLY decides whether the 2020-vintage f-pin plausibly transfers to 2025-S1 on the f-axis; it does NOT compute a hazard, does NOT pin s_usd, and does NOT resolve the single-snapshot temporal degeneracy (DP5 paths a, d). It bounds ONE judgment -- how far f must fall to break identification (f*=0.249), how far 2020 f is from it (0.28), and whether that fall is plausible given 14 years of history (it is not) -- and makes that judgment evidenced rather than bare.
